description:
Use Firefox to analyze the archived MDN homepage, extract the computed typography for the main <h1> heading, capture an isolated screenshot of that heading, and capture a full mobile viewport screenshot.

Goal: Extract computed CSS styles and responsive screenshots for UI analysis.

Actions:
1. Firefox is already open to an archived MDN page (https://web.archive.org/web/20230921054336/https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/).
2. Create a new directory at ~/Documents/UI_Analysis/.
3. Locate the primary <h1> heading ("Resources for developers, by developers"). Do not inspect the Wayback Machine header.
4. Use Firefox Developer Tools to extract the Computed font-family and font-size for this <h1>.
5. Save these computed values in ~/Documents/UI_Analysis/typography.txt.
6. Use the DevTools Inspector's built-in feature to capture an isolated node screenshot of just that <h1> element. Save/move it to ~/Documents/UI_Analysis/heading_component.png.
7. Activate Firefox's Responsive Design Mode.
8. Set the viewport exactly to 375 x 812 with a Device Pixel Ratio (DPR) of 1.
9. Capture a screenshot of the responsive viewport using the built-in RDM screenshot tool. Save/move it to ~/Documents/UI_Analysis/mobile_viewport.png.
